summary: Global markets are navigating a cautious, risk-off mood as geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns weigh heavily on investor sentiment, though U.S. equities are attempting to extend a six-week winning streak in the S&P 500. The Iran conflict remains the dominant macro driver, with the German economy already flagging Q2 damage, European shares tumbling on war-linked inflation fears, and global bonds selling off sharply as the 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.1%, its highest level in nearly a year. In individual stocks, AI chipmakers remain in focus with Jim Cramer calling for trims in a volatile name while Cerebras attracted attention following its IPO, and Palantir drew scrutiny after Trump publicly touted the stock on Truth Social following personal purchases, raising ethical concerns. The primary risks investors are monitoring include stalled Iran nuclear negotiations — with Tehran signaling deep distrust of Washington — unresolved BRICS divisions over the Iran war, and uncertainty around Trump's pending decision on sanctions targeting Chinese companies purchasing Iranian oil, which could significantly escalate trade and geopolitical friction. In the near term, bond market pressure from sticky inflation, persistent geopolitical instability, and stretched equity valuations suggest a fragile backdrop, with any escalation in the Iran conflict or sanctions announcement capable of quickly reversing the S&P 500's recent momentum.
